response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators), but the role is fundamentally a people-management position and the resume shows no formal people-management or team-leadership experience. There is no evidence of conducting one-on-ones, performance management, roadmap ownership, or leading organizational change. Mobile exposure is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint a decade ago, well short of the role's mobile-platform ecosystem focus.
strengths: Deep hands-on developer-tools experience: test runners (Trial), assertion libraries, CI servers, GitLab administration, and MCP code generators
Broad technical range across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development)
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience; role requires 2+ years managing teams
No demonstrated technical leadership of teams or organizational change at the scope the role demands
Mobile-platform experience is shallow and dated (one iOS/Apple TV role in 2015-2016)

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
